howItWorks.intro: This dog calorie calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How are dog calories calculated?
faq.answer: We use the Resting Energy Requirement (RER) formula: 70 × (body weight in kg)^0.75, then multiply by an activity/life stage factor.

faq.question: How much should I feed my dog?
faq.answer: Follow calorie guidelines rather than cup measurements, as kibble calorie density varies. Check your food's calories per cup and divide daily needs accordingly.

faq.question: How do I know if my dog is overweight?
faq.answer: You should feel ribs easily without pressing hard. A visible waist when viewed from above and a tucked abdomen from the side indicate healthy weight.

faq.question: Should I feed once or twice a day?
faq.answer: Adult dogs do well with 2 meals per day. Puppies need 3-4 meals. Large breeds prone to bloat may benefit from 2-3 smaller meals rather than one large one.
